ads.author: Randy Womac
ads.title: Restored Data East Maverick Pinball
ads.description: This Maverick was restored by me four years ago and has been a showpiece in my shop, Randy’s Pinball, pretty much ever since. I and others have played and thoroughly enjoyed this game for years now. I keep it maintained impeccably and it will have a fresh coat of wax and new glass before it leaves my shop. The MPU and PPB have been professionally rebuilt and future proofed. The power supply is new. It has a Pinsound custom sound card and a large format Color DMD. Everything on the playfield is new, the plastics, the ramp, the pop bumpers, the boat. All of it. Everything under the playfield is either new or has been completely refurbished. If you know anything about me, you know I take great pride in my work. This is no exception. This one needs nothing and had no expense spared. However, it is a good deal for the $3500 worth of parts and work that has been put into it. $4600 and this will be your new favorite pinball machine.

It is equipped with LED lighting throughout the cabinet and playfield. The artwork is by Doug Watson and the game design is by Brian Eddy. The playfield features multiple ramps, targets, and a magnetic ball lock mechanism.

ads.author: Chris Marquette
ads.title: RUSH Limited Edition
ads.description: FOR SALE: Stern RUSH Limited Edition (LE) Pinball Machine Price: $9495 Condition: Great Condition 7200 Lifetime Plays Location: SUNBURY, PA, 17801 "Fly By Night" no more—bring the legendary rock trio right into your home! I am selling my gorgeous Stern RUSH Limited Edition (LE) pinball machine. This is number 846 out of only 1,000 ever made. Designed by the legendary John Borg, this machine is a fast-flowing, music-packed masterpiece that is an absolute must-have for both serious pinball collectors and hardcore Rush fans. This machine has been meticulously. It plays flawlessly, looks practically brand new, and is packed with all the exclusive factory LE features. Limited Edition Exclusive Features: Production Plaque: Custom serialized LE plaque (# 846 / 1000). Exclusive Artwork: Stunning LE-only cabinet artwork and mirrored backglass. Custom Armor: Upgraded, metallic powder-coated armor, legs, hinges, and lockdown bar. Expression Lighting SystemTM: Immersive cabinet lighting that syncs perfectly to the music. Upgraded Audio: Upgraded speaker system to blast the 16 iconic Rush tracks. Shaker Motor: Factory-installed shaker motor for tactile feedback during gameplay. Game Highlights: 16 full-length studio master tracks (including Tom Sawyer, The Spirit of Radio, Working Man, and more). Custom speech recorded exclusively for the game by Geddy Lee, Alex Lifeson, and Ed Robertson (Barenaked Ladies). "Time Machine" electromagnetic ball lock, custom sculpted drum kit, and ramp diverters. Logistics & Payment: Payment: Cash on the glass or wire transfer only. No checks, no scams. Trades: [e.g., Not looking for trades at this time. Pickup: Easy, ground-floor pickup. I can help you break it down and load it into your vehicle. Shipping: [e.g., Local pickup heavily preferred, but willing Pack,Pallet, and Ship possibly. Message me with any questions or to set up a time to come play it.

ads.author: Chris Cannoe
ads.title: Vintage Arcade Game Cabinets - Dragon's Lair II, Space Ace, Galaga
ads.description: Classic arcade cabinets with original side art. These units are constructed from wood and laminate with black finishes. The collection includes titles such as Dragon's Lair II: Time Warp by Leland Corporation, the original Dragon's Lair, Space Ace, and Galaga. Each cabinet has its respective joystick and button controls. Selling the last of my games. I have six left with extra parts. First up is a Ms. Pac-Man/ Galaga reunion in a caberet cabinet. It will also play Pac-Man if you do the secret code to unlock it. Game is in very good shape and an extra 6 power supplies. It worked perfect a couple years ago but now it seems like it needs a cap kit. I am also including an lcd monitor with converter board in case you like to make it work like that instead. Second is a multicade with like 60 different games that are vertically oriented and features a trackball as well for some of the games. You could see most of the games in the photos. This game works great. Third game is a Dragons Lair II. This game I purchased off the arcade floor back in 94. They had several of them and I chose the one that looked the best. It is currently working on all original parts and nothing on it is reproduction. I also have two extra Sony 1450 laser players included. One was working when put in storage and the other was almost working. Currently the game works perfectly. Next up is Dragons Lair. I purchased this one from the same arcade in 86. One of my first games that I owned. It is all original and all parts inside and out are as I purchased it almost 45 years ago. The game has been solid and has never had any issues. When I purchased it they included extra sets of ROMs. One set plays the game in story mode. I have never seen this in the arcade machine before only on the home version. Also included are a set of space ace roms with the adapter harness to play in this cabinet, and an extra dragons lair set. Fifth game is a dedicated space ace. Again this one is all original and nothing on it is reproduced. This machine I purchased in 2000 and it was supposedly only one owner. This one is super clean and works perfect. Also included is an extra score board that is supposed to work. Last up is an original ultracade. As you can see in the pictures there are many games on this. It does look like the original monitor was replaced with an lcd screen. Game seems to work perfect and has extra game packs installed. Including the leisure pack with dl,dl2 and space ace, the midway treasures pack, and the williams pack. One thing I don’t like is the bezel cover is very cloudy and may need to be replaced. The laser games also all have their original manuals. I am looking for selling all games in one transaction and buyer will need to arrange any packing and shipping. Free pickup however. $15000

ads.author: Tim Walters
ads.title: Zaccaria Locomotion Pinball Machine
ads.description: Here is a machine you very seldom see in the US. Let alone one that has been freshly overhauled. Here are the major highlights of what this machine has received over the past 8 months: 1. Cabinet - new back panel plywood, new backbox mount, new transformer plywood platform, new rear leg mounts. Sanded cabinet to bare wood, filled imperfections, primed, painted, custom art decals designed, printed and installed. Hardware & all metal cleaned and polished, coin door cleaned and polished, metal cabinet leg protectors installed along with new chrome legs, bolts and adjusters. 2. Backbox - sanded, filled, primed and painted. Display door painted white. Hardware and metal cleaned and polished. New non-mirrored backglass with new lift channel and trim. 3. Electronics - all boards (MPU, driver, power, sound, six display assemblies) cleaned, inspected and repaired as necessary. Suspect solder joints and contact pins have been reflowed. The MPU board has between 8-12 new chips with sockets. Driver has several new transistors, SCRs and caps installed. Display assemblies have several new transistors and several non-new, but fully functional display modules. 4. Electrical - harnesses and connectors have been cleaned and inspected. Some connectors have been serviced and previous wiring splices have been refreshed. Transformers, relays, lamp sockets, have been cleaned and serviced as necessary. New ground braid installed in cabinet and between cabinet and backbox. Also installed a new speaker. 5. Playfield - complete top and underside teardown. All solenoids, switches, brackets and mechanicals (including drop target assemblies) cleaned and adjusted (or replaced) as necessary. Almost all new fasteners (screws and bolts) used for reassembly. Some artwork touched up with mylar installed over the touchups (mainly just a small area right above the flippers - overall it was already a pretty nice playfield). All new plastic posts, rubber rings, and new Comet sunlight LEDs (GI and non-ghosting inserts). Several plastics have been repaired and reinforced. New apron scorecards and labels. I feel like I may have missed mentioning a few things. But that’s the bulk of the work that I did over the past seven months. The machine is now set up in my family room/office and I’ve been playing it to get everything dialed in and ensuring it is reliable. I actually bought two basket case machines and created one really nice machine out of the two. I thought about restoring the second machine, but one was enough for me. I’ll be selling that one off as a major project and/or for spare parts. Only reason I mention that machine is that it could be bundled for sale/trade with this one if you’re interested in having lots of spare parts. The machine is located in White Oak, PA. I’d prefer a buyer who will come see it, inspect it, and play it before purchase. But I do have walk around and game play videos for those who may not be able to come see it. Although it would break my heart to have it damaged in some way during shipping, I will accommodate the shipper of your choice as long as they come to the house and pick it up. It’s on the main floor so there are no steps up or down. As for the sale, I’m asking $6400. I’m also open to trade offers for similar value pins. Drop me a message if you’d like to discuss. Thanks, Tim

ads.author: Ashley Day
ads.title: Jersey Jack Godfather 50th Collector's Edition pinball machine.
ads.description: AN OFFER YOU MAY FIND VERY DIFFICULT TO REFUSE For sale is my **Jersey Jack Pinball — The Godfather 50th Anniversary Collector’s Edition**, individually numbered **#959 of only 1,000 produced worldwide**. This is not merely a pinball machine. This is a 400-pound, gold-trimmed, Tommy-gun-firing monument to the Corleone family—and it has decided the time has come to join a new family. THE MACHINE • Collector’s Edition #959 of 1,000 • Home Use Only — HUO • Only 490 total plays at the time of this listing • Plays and operates basically like new • Well cared for, regularly cleaned and waxed • Still in my personal playing rotation • Available for serious buyers to inspect and play before purchasing The play count may increase slightly because I am still enjoying it, and potential buyers are welcome to test it. I refuse to make a machine this spectacular sit silently in a corner like it has entered witness protection. COLLECTOR’S EDITION EXCLUSIVES This is Jersey Jack’s top-tier, sold-out Collector’s Edition—not the Limited Edition. It features: • Interactive topper with two rotating, Tommy-gun-wielding thugs • Dual mechanical knockers that bring the topper’s gunfire to life • Hand-painted car and New York skyline topper scene • Golden mirrored backglass surrounding the massive 27-inch display • Golden wireform ramps • Custom laser-cut gold armor • Deep burgundy legs • Golden Corleone lion lockbar sculpture • Removable magnetic golden lion leg sculptures • Sculpted horse-head shooter knob with golden shooter rod • Exclusive Collector’s Edition inner art blades • Mirror-effect cabinet artwork • 3D Statue of Liberty with an RGB-lit torch • Motorized “Big Mike” Caporegime sculpt with illuminated gun • Don Vito Corleone sculpted bust • More than 450 individually controlled playfield RGB LEDs • More than 260 individually controlled exterior cabinet RGB LEDs • 27-inch display, Invisiglass, shaker motor, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and interactive high-score camera • Deep, strategic gameplay built around controlling territories, recruiting soldiers and taking down the other families There are more shots, toys, lights, ramps, sculptures and glorious pinball insanity packed into this machine than I can reasonably describe without requiring Facebook to add a second Marketplace page. CONDITION The machine is in excellent, well-maintained HUO condition and plays beautifully. There are a few **very minor dings on the inner art blades**, which are shown in the pictures. I want that disclosed clearly because nobody appreciates discovering a surprise after the deal—unless the surprise is a cannoli. Beyond those small cosmetic marks, this machine has been treated with respect, kept clean, waxed and properly cared for. INCLUDED The machine comes with **all of the extras that originally came with it**, as shown in the pictures. Please review the photos carefully, as they are part of the description and show the machine, included extras and the minor art-blade imperfections. THE TRANSACTION • Come see it • Come inspect it • Come play it • Cash on the glass • Easy load-out • I will gladly help load it if needed Please bring an appropriate vehicle, proper equipment and enough capable help to transport a full-size Jersey Jack pinball machine safely. I will help, but I am not assembling a last-minute Corleone moving crew using one guy, a bath towel and a Honda Civic. Serious inquiries are welcome. Lowball offers may be presented to the Five Families for review, but their approval process is notoriously unforgiving. This is your opportunity to own **The Godfather Collector’s Edition #959 of 1,000**—a spectacular playing machine, an incredible display piece and one of the most gloriously over-the-top pinball packages Jersey Jack has ever produced. Come play it, make your decision, put the cash on the glass and take home an offer your game room cannot refuse.

New Weebly MPU installed. Power supply board has new capacitors and recommended ground modifications. New rectifier board. Game is set for free play. Price is $2750. No trades. Easy loading from garage.

ads.description: Elvis Pinball Machine by Stern – $7,700 This game I see s blast for any Elvis fan. Sing along his classics as you open the HEARTBREAK HOTEL put your BLUE SUEDE SHOES & go multi ball as you sing JAILHOUSE ROCK and feel the machine vibrate as Elvis is ALL SHOOK UP and he shimmy’s down the playing field. One-owner, smoke free home-use only Stern Elvis Pinball Machine. Purchased new in 2004 and has never been in a commercial location or arcade. Adult-owned, well cared for, and in excellent working condition. Excellent condition, recently replaced all 4 flipper rubbers and lower rubber rails. The remainder of the Elvis rubber replace kit is included. Features authentic Elvis recordings, Hound Dog, Jailhouse Rock, Heartbreak Hotel, All Shook Up and more. The animated dancing Elvis figure, bright playfield, and three additional Elvis figurines professionally added to the playfield, making it a unique one-of-a-kind machine. This machine has spent its entire life in my home and has seen very light use. Includes original manual and keys. Local pickup only One owner since new (2004) Home use only Excellent condition Includes three additional Elvis figurines Clean cabinet and playfield Local pickup only $7,700 If you're an Elvis fan or pinball collector looking for a beautiful home-use example, this machine won't disappoint

ads.author: Dynel Matvia
ads.title: (PENDING) 1988 "Taxi" fully restored pinball machine, works great
ads.description: My father was an electrician in the steel mill for over 42 years. He specialized in circuit boards, cpu boards and soundboards. He has done every board that you could possibly think of for electrical work. He had been fully restoring and completely renovating to its original form of every pinball machine that he worked on , which was so many that I could lost count years ago.. He was very meticulous and particular about perfection on each of his machines than he did for people. Pinball machines were his pride and joy as his side job from the mill on top of that , he also could build classic cars from the ground up. He was definitely a workaholic, but he loved it.Even until 2 days he went into the hospital.He was still working on pinball machines, All the way up until then. Taxi is definitely unique and definitely fun. I have it set on free play. Comes with 4 free new legs that are universal legs. This was one of his favorite machines because it is the first machine he worked on and fully restored when he came to florida from indiana because he had his own side business called rick's pinball in indiana. My dad also did a lot of work for a very well-known pinball arcade owner in the Fort Myers area. And they were very close. This machine is from 1988, made by Williams. And has marilyn, Lola, and Santa and a vampire, so it's kind of a comedy, but it's a very fun game, probably one of the funnest games I've ever played. My dad changed all the lights that originally came with the machine to LED lights.He does that with all his machines before he died in two thousand twenty four from cancer. I really hate to have to get rid of this machine but I've seen a lot of pinball machines in my lifetime and I think it's time to let it go, but it was his last machine that he owned, and it meant a lot to him. So I'm asking $3,500. I will post the price Range from pin side and I'm willing to negotiate a small amount, but not much. Because it is in perfect condition, he also redid the cabinet. All the boards are perfect. I mean, like pristine, because that's what he specialized in, so I am located in Lehigh Acres east, kind of near Pelican, snowballs and Richmond sort of 6th street. I do have some extra boards here. Depending on what boards they are, some may just come with it and some may cost a little, but some of the boards might be extras for parts if you might ever need them. And I also have a small bag of pinball parts of these random things that you might need in LED lights. So if you're interested, please let me know if I don't get to backseat to you right away, I will be back to you as soon as I see it like I said, asking 3500, the value is posted in the pics, but you're also getting any kind of parts you can find in my house and 4 new legs. and I know it's going to be very hard to ever find Marilyn on a pinball machine without her permission, so I actually heard that they got in trouble for doing that so So that's definitely a collector. Im home all day today. God bless.

This machine is all original with the original 25 inch ampliphone color vector monitor, new trim, new plexiglass, new graphics and paint, etc. the board was sent out and repaired and has zero issues. The count door and mechs are all rebuilt too, although it’s set to free play at the moment. The sound is amazing too. I will consider partial trades for other machines (especially if I can obtain an upright Star Wars). I’m only interested in classic arcade machines (nothing past around 1985/86). I’ve priced this aggressively for how amazing the condition is. I’m sure I’m likely to get bombarded with inquiries due to how rare this grail piece is. I honestly never thought I’d part with it when I finally found one, because I probably won’t find another one like it, but I just moved into a new home with a slightly more challenging setup for my arcade machines. I will assist with shipping and could help with loading. I may be able to locate and load it onto a pallet if shipping is necessary as well.

ads.author: Eric Sturdivant
ads.title: Alive Pinball Machine
ads.description: Alive is a solid-state pinball machine produced by Brunswick Manufacturing Co. in 1978. It was designed specifically as an inexpensive "home use only" model rather than a commercial arcade machine, which is reflected in its smaller cabinet size and lack of a coin slot.The "Elvis" ConnectionThe machine is most famous for its unofficial association with Elvis Presley.Design Background: It was originally intended to be an Elvis-themed machine. However, after Elvis died in 1977, Brunswick reportedly could not secure the rights to his name or image.The "Look-alike": To circumvent legal issues, the Artist's Depiction on the backglass features an Elvis-like figure with a hairstyle more reminiscent of Tom Jones, another popular 1970s icon.Gameplay and FeaturesElectronic Scoring: It features digital displays for up to four players and includes electronic scoring sounds and seven rock-style songs.Playfield Layout: The relatively simple field includes two flippers, one "thumper-bumper" (pop bumper), a manual plunger, and a spinner.Targets: Players can aim for specific rollover switches and targets to earn double or triple bonus points.Build Quality: Because it was a home model, it was built with lighter materials like plywood and "sawdust and glue" rather than heavy-duty arcade-grade wood. Recently Serviced

ads.author: Mike Mackey
ads.title: Atari Kangaroo Arcade Game – Beautiful Cabinet – $600
ads.description: For Sale: Offered for sale is a very clean, original Atari Kangaroo upright arcade game. The cabinet presents beautifully. The interior has been thoroughly cleaned, and the entire wire harness has been cleaned and detailed. The marquee glass and outer bezel glass are both in excellent condition with no paint flaking. The only cosmetic detraction is that the coin door could use a fresh coat of paint. The game features its original monitor, which has been fully recapped, including a new filter capacitor, producing a bright, crisp picture with excellent color. The regulator/audio board has been completely rebuilt, and the power brick/power supply section has also been rebuilt, including a new Big Blue filter capacitor and bridge rectifier, making the monitor and power systems ready for years of reliable service. After the rebuilds, the game played perfectly with excellent picture quality and sound. It currently has a startup issue where it usually boots to the familiar "jail bars" screen instead of the game. On the occasions that it boots correctly, it plays flawlessly and continues to run without issue. Based on the troubleshooting completed so far, the intermittent Jail bar issue appears to be isolated to the game PCB stack (CPU/video boards) rather than the rebuilt monitor or power section. At this point, I'm simply ready to move on to other projects. Video showing the game running correctly, followed by the startup jail bar issue: https://youtu.be/s2AsnePrE2A?is=7U1lotOc85PwkjrG Price: $600 FIRM Pickup: Nicholasville, Kentucky (warehouse location across from Sam's Club) This is an excellent opportunity to own one of Atari's classic early-1980s arcade games. Over two weeks of restoration work has already been invested. The cabinet has been cleaned inside and out, the wiring harness has been detailed, the monitor has been fully recapped (including the filter capacitor), and both the regulator/audio board and power brick have been rebuilt. The remaining issue appears to be confined to the game electronics, making this an excellent project for an arcade hobbyist or technician looking to finish a very nice example. I would prefer not to part it out, although I could easily make more money doing so. I'd much rather see this machine restored and enjoyed. At $600, It's an excellent value.

ads.author: Adam Walton
ads.title: 1993 GOTTLIEB STREET FIGHTER II VINTAGE PINBALL GAME
ads.description: *IF ITS LISTED ITS AVAILABLE* The Street Fighter II Pinball Machine is a collaboration between Premier/Gottlieb and Capcom, emerging onto the scene in early 1993. Born from the iconic video game franchise, this pinball sensation is a true collector’s delight. With a limited production run of just 5,550 units, this solid state electronic marvel boasts an array of features that catapult it into the realm of classic gaming. The playfield showcases five flippers, including one that intriguingly resides below the playfield and another that gracefully performs a 360-degree rotation. In this three-level playfield, two ramps shine, each unveiling an elevating entrance to allow ball access beneath. As the heart of the action, multiball mode steals the show, offering both 2-ball and 3-ball dynamics. Among the enticing attractions is a smashable car nestled within the lower playfield The objective of the Street Fighter II Pinball Machine is to conquer a succession of twelve opponents, each linked to a specific shot that must be expertly executed when the opponent’s name is illuminated by a white light. The thrill deepens as three opponents are constantly illuminated at the outset, starting with the formidable trio of E. Honda, M. Bison, and Sagat. Victory over each opponent unveils a tantalizing array of selectable prizes, offering players a dynamic and immersive journey. Embrace the nostalgia, the challenge, and the exhilaration of the Street Fighter II Pinball Machine—a testament to the power of collaboration between two iconic realms of gaming. Game is located at our store: Walton's Games 4772 Ritter Ave Blue Ash, Ohio 45242 Pick up in store for free. We can deliver & setup within the Cincy Metro for $150 We are a business - all sales subject to 7.8% Ohio sales tax. Shipping available at buyers expense. Contact us for a shipping quote: 513-281-4444

ads.author: Rebecca Gabel
ads.title: 1963 Corvette Cross County Bally Pinball WORKING, UNRESTORED
ads.description: 1963 Bally Cross Country Pinball Machine – Original & Working – Only 500 Produced Offered for sale is an exceptionally rare 1963 Bally Cross Country electro-mechanical pinball machine. Designed by the legendary Ted Zale, Cross Country was Bally’s second flipper game after returning to the flipper market in 1963 and is widely recognized as Ted Zale’s first original Bally design. With a documented production run of just 500 machines, it is one of the scarcest early Bally EM games and is seldom offered for sale. This machine is working and retains its original character, making it an outstanding addition to any serious Bally or early electro-mechanical collection. Features: * Manufactured by Bally in March 1963 * Original Ted Zale design * Only 500 produced * Electro-mechanical score reels * Original artwork and classic early Bally gameplay * Fully working Whether you’re a collector of early Bally machines, a Ted Zale enthusiast, or simply appreciate historically significant pinball machines, opportunities to acquire a Cross Country are few and far between. The machine presents well, plays as it should, and is ready to enjoy or display. Additional photos, gameplay video, and specific condition details are available upon request. Serious inquiries are welcome.

ads.author: Mike Mackey
ads.title: Nintendo HELIFIRE 1980 arcade cocktail game - ONLY 2017 Plays
ads.description: (Located near Lexington, KY) VERY RARE cocktail version of game only 2017 plays on coin counter. Nintendo HeliFire is rarely found in the wild—it’s the rarest of Nintendo’s early video arcade lineup. It came out after Radar Scope, both released in 1980, and prior to Donkey Kong, the game that made Nintendo famous. HeliFire had the “TUB-up” designation stamped on both the cabinet top plate and the monitor frame. This example has both, so it’s definitely a legit Nintendo build and not a conversion. And far rarer, than the already much sought after upright cabinet; because cocktail are produced in a much smaller percentage than uprights. Everything is original. Has switching power supply installed, but includes the original power supply as well. All boards have been serviced: socketed chips removed and legs cleaned, all tantalum caps replaced, nearly all electrolytic caps replaced. Monitor has been recapped. It has run for multiple hours at a time in attract mode and in play, both to burn in and to flush out any potential issues with the servicing. No problems have occurred. There are only 13 acknowledged owners of HeliFire in the VAPS (Video Arcade Preservation Society) census (combined uprights and cocktails). The VAPS numbers are a bit confusing because they only specify member activity from the past three years. Current VAPS stats list 3 uprights and 3 cocktails. Of course, there are likely more examples out in the wild, as not everyone records their serial numbers with VAPS. Walkthrough vid here: https://youtu.be/7-H8C4wyWbQ?si=xUoLG-4BUlTYWABm I will palletize for $30. I have palletized and shipped numerous arcade/pinball games with no issues, and my palletizing is far better than any shipper. Guaranteed secure payment requested (cash, bank wire transfer or equivalent). Pick-up available at our Winchester, KY warehouse. FaceTime walk-through available upon request. Any questions are welcome.

ads.description: I purchased this cabinet kit for a Time Warp restoration project, but I ended up selling the machine before beginning the rebuild. The cabinet was professionally CNC-cut by Kirk Weaver (NW Ohio) using high-quality cabinet-grade plywood. The cuts are clean and accurate, and the kit is ready for assembly. Includes: Complete unassembled cabinet Complete unassembled backbox/head Internal wood rails, braces, and trim pieces Not included: Hardware/fasteners Metal brackets Coin door Legs Lockdown receiver Any game components This is an excellent starting point for restoring a Williams standard-body game such as: Time Warp Flash Phoenix Tri Zone Stellar Wars Algar Laser Cue Blackout Gorgar Firepower (and other compatible Williams standard-body machines) I drove to Ohio to pick this up specifically for my project, but my plans changed after selling the game. My loss is your gain. Located in Fishers, Indiana. Due to the size, local pickup only. If you're restoring a System 4/6/7 Williams game and don't want to repair a damaged cabinet, this is a great opportunity to start with a brand-new, precision-cut replacement.

ads.author: Mike Donato
ads.title: Bally Theatre of Magic high end restoration
ads.description: This Bally Theatre of Magic has undergone an extensive restoration by Robert Keller. The cabinet is a brand new screen printed cabinet from Doug House, let's play pinball. Playfield is an original which was sent out to Brian Weissmann of HSA Pinball playfield restorations where it was restored and clear coated. All plastics, ramps, and posts are new. All devices have been rebuilt. This pin only has 117 plays on it since the restoration and is in like new condition. Everything is dialed in and functioning as it should. The only negative to report at all would be that due to the tight tolerance between the new cabinet and playfield there is some scraping inside of the cabinet and also some very minor damage to the print under the legs. This is an opportunity to own a truly exceptional rendition of this game. Trade ins are excepted on very good condition pins at reasonable trade in values. Willing to ship nationwide at buyers expense.

ads.author: Greg Rodig
ads.title: Williams Police Force Pinball
ads.description: Players condition police. Everything works and plays as it should. Positives -Cabinet is solid and isn’t to faded. Reds still look nice. No funky smells water damage or cracks in the cabinet. Inside is very clean. -Playfield in above average condition compared to others. Shooter lane is clean and no insert wear that many other playfields have. Does have ball drop wear on the right in lane and a playfield flaw in front of the drop targets next to the center ramp. -All boards are clean. No acid damage. Game holds memory. -Game is LED’ed and was shopped out at some point in the past. -All displays work with no digits out. -Car works as do all the lights, sounds etc. -No cracks in in plastics. -Needs new locks as well as top channel for trans light. -Drop targets need to be cleaned and they drop slowly. -Displays need new double sided tape on the back. Can help wrap and load, in my garage for easy loading. $2,500 Williams Bally Stern System 11 Gottlieb JJP Jersey Jack Pinball

Details: * Powers on and game loads * Monitor displays and game is playable * Original dual gun setup * Cabinet is in good overall condition * Gun controls/buttons need repair * Sold AS-IS At $300, it’s priced well below the value of a fully working cabinet and is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys arcade restoration. Located in Twin Falls, Idaho. Bring help to load. We have a dolly to help.
